issue_title: Reduce number of coverage comments on PRs to one
issue_body:
Currently, the coveralls service posts a number of comments for the initial PR and for each update of a PR. All these comments from one "run" provide the same link.

The number of such comments for the same "run" and link is the number of successful Travis jobs. Starting with PR #172, the Travis CI runs for Python 2.7, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6, and pypy. The Travis control file invokes coveralls at the end upon success. In all five cases, the Travis run succeeds up to the point where coveralls runs. For the python x.y versions, coveralls itself also succeeds, each one resulting in a comment on the PR. For pypy, coveralls is attempted to be run, but fails with an exception, so no coverage comment is posted for that environment.

Some investigation on the coveralls.io links: The link in the little badge in each coveralls comment targets a "build" of Travis (e.g. https://coveralls.io/builds/9852638 for Travis build 820). A "build" is the set of all Travis Python environments in a particular PR. The run for each Travis Python environment within a PR is termed a "job". There are also links to coveralls.io pages for those jobs (e.g. https://coveralls.io/jobs/22316159 for Travis job 820.1 within Travis build 820). The coveralls.io page for the build also has the links for the job pages.

Bottom line, it would be sufficient to have exactly one posted coveralls comment with the link it currently has (to the page for the "build"), and not four of them.

This issue is to reduce the number of coverage comments that are posted by the coveralls service for a particular run/build of a PR, to one.

### Describe the problem

We've observed an issue in iOS Safari (tested in 11.2) where icons are rendering normally, then unload and reload again (causing a box flash) as the page finishes refreshing. The relevant CSS and JS files are cached properly and we do not observe this in any other browser. The icons in question are using pseudo-elements if that helps narrow it down.

### What did you expect?

Pseudo-element icons should not unload and re-render on page refresh when cached properly.

### What version and implementation are you using?

Version: Font Awesome Pro 5.0.6
Browser and version: iOS Safari v11.2

- [x] SVG with JS
- [ ] Web Fonts with CSS
- [ ] SVG Sprites

.header.ses-content.content .nav-toggle:before {

}

- [x] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate

I'm testing recently added JSONB.

Setup:
```
python3.6
psycopg2==2.7.4
SQLAlchemy==1.2.5
cockroachdb v2.0.1
```

I was able to save a sample data into the database. This is how it looks in a dump file:
```
{
    "id" : "2a4b4578-1a5d-455b-9317-c90c5f3afe15",
    "enabled" : true,
    "language" : "en-us",
    "ui_metadata" : "{\"origin\": \"111\", \"ui\": null, \"version\": null}",
}
```
I'm now loading data with sqlalchemy session.query.all()
Error:
```
    return json_deserializer(value)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/json/__init__.py", line 348, in loads
    'not {!r}'.format(s.__class__.__name__))
TypeError: the JSON object must be str, bytes or bytearray, not 'dict'
```
https://bitbucket.org/zzzeek/sqlalchemy/src/7541d9b258e4d0c115d6160592ac7e17f8fd50cc/lib/sqlalchemy/sql/sqltypes.py?at=master&fileviewer=file-view-default#sqltypes.py-2145:2155
https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroachdb-python/blob/master/cockroachdb/sqlalchemy/dialect.py

This line fails:
```
json_deserializer = dialect._json_deserializer or json.loads
```
Because there is no `_json_deserializer` and it tries to call json.loads on a python dict.

issue_title: Option to save start and end times for songs.
issue_body:
Many music videos have some sort of scene at the beginning before the actual song starts and using official music videos seems to be the best way to ensure a YouTube video doesn't get taken down for copyright infringement.

If we could have an option to tell a song saved to a playlist to always start at a certain second that would help solve the problem. (The same issue sometimes applies for the end of a music video as well)

As an example the actual song in Taylor Swifts Style doesn't start until 10 seconds in.  Roar by Katy Perry doesn't start until 9 seconds in and the song ends at 4:01 with the video being 4:29 long. You get the idea.

issue_title: [Bug report] iter_neighborhood() gives incorrect neighborhoods for both von Neumann and Moore
issue_body:
Moore's neighborhood is defined as:
![Moore neighborhood](http://mathworld.wolfram.com/images/equations/MooreNeighborhood/NumberedEquation1.gif)
Or see [this link](http://mathworld.wolfram.com/MooreNeighborhood.html) for a more complete explanation.

In the code however, it is calculated by excluding everything that is outside the Euclidean distance:
```Py
if moore and (dy ** 2 + dx ** 2) ** .5 > radius > 1:
    continue
```
This can be corrected by just removing the condition, as the correct indices are already visited.


Von Neumann's (extended) neighborhood is defined as:
![von Neumann neighborhood](http://mathworld.wolfram.com/images/equations/vonNeumannNeighborhood/NumberedEquation1.gif)
Or see [this link](http://mathworld.wolfram.com/vonNeumannNeighborhood.html) for a more complete explanation.

In the code however, it is calculated by excluding any diagonals, which makes it more like a huge cross:
```Py
if not moore and dy != 0 and dx != 0:
    continue
```
This can be corrected as follows:
```Py
if not moore and abs(dx) + abs(dy) > radius:
    continue
```

I need to run a consumer in an OS thread. I want to communicate with a queue. curio tells me I should use their UniversalQueue, the purpose being that it blocks correctly from within curio and from within the thread.

However, 3.5/3.6 compat is an issue, and if it wasn't for that I would've PR'd in already (since the change is probably super trivial, `msg = task.coro.send(next_send)` -> `context = contextvars.copy_context(); msg = context.run(task.coro.send, next_send)` or similar.), and I don't know how that would be solved.

issue_title: Convert Python tests from unittest to pytest.
Import error GLIBCXX_3.4.21 not found when importing numbuf.
issue_body:
I followed the installation instructions on a fresh Ubuntu VM using Anaconda with Python 3 (though I am pretty sure I have seen this error with Python 2 as well).

Ray builds and installs fine. However, if I try to run `runtest.py` (or simply import numbuf), I get the following error.

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test/runtest.py", line 6, in <module>
    import ray
  File "/home/ubuntu/ray/lib/python/ray/__init__.py", line 16, in <module>
    import ray.serialization
  File "/home/ubuntu/ray/lib/python/ray/serialization.py", line 6, in <module>
    import numbuf
  File "/home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/numbuf-0.0.1-py3.5.egg/numbuf/__init__.py", line 5, in <module>
    from numbuf.libnumbuf import *
ImportError: /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/bin/../lib/libstdc++.so.6: version `GLIBCXX_3.4.21' not found (required by /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/numbuf-0.0.1-py3.5.egg/numbuf/libnumbuf.so)
```

I followed one of the solutions here http://askubuntu.com/questions/575505/glibcxx-3-4-20-not-found-how-to-fix-this-error, which suggested running the following.

```
conda install libgcc
```

That fixed the problem.

Should we specifically look for this error and print something more helpful? Or should we change the installation instructions to mention this potential problem?

issue_title: Add support for Open Graph Arrays
issue_body:
It is possible in Open Graph Protocol to specify more than one value for a single property. It's called OG Array http://ogp.me/#array. 

It seems that currently `extruct` doesn't support arrays in case the `uniform` option is set to `True`, because `uniform._uopengraph` function doesn't handle duplicated keys from the list of properties.

It'd be cool to add that support and return list in case there is more than one value or to just add some separate property with `list` suffix to be backward compatible.

我向来是不用这些的，但是没有办法只能就范。

效果完美。
access http://slideshare.net redirect circulation

issue_title: shadowsocks远程dns解析bug，翻墙网址会向本地dns提交dns解析，本地dns会返回错误的ip，从而会记录谁几时登陆了某网址！
issue_body:
我不清楚是shadowsocks还是SwitchyOmega的问题，所以向两位开发者都提交这个bug。

shadowsocks-dns远程解析bug，翻墙网址会向本地dns提交dns解析，本地dns会返回错误的ip，从而会记录谁几时登陆了某网址！
试验环境，
1，A机器xp，原版，
2，安装有wireshark，截获dns数据
3，本地设置114.114.114.114dns
4，chrome浏览器，安装SwitchyOmega，设置127.0.0.1:65500的socks5代理
5，shadowsocks代理。
截获步骤：
一、
1，无论设置SwitchyOmega自动代理，gfwed，登陆youtube.com，youtube可以打开，说明选择了正确的远程dns解析ip
2，wireshark，抓取数据包。
3，wireshark，分析中包含，有114返回的错误ip
![123](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10794158/5991448/3b712acc-aa2b-11e4-9299-921960c241c1.jpg)

结论：说明浏览器向远程ip和本地dns都发送了dns解析请求，但是只选择了远程的dns解析ip，但是同时本地的114上会有，某时间点本机所在外网ip请求了youtube。com的记录。如果倒查就爆菊了。

如果某人不想让某人知道自己上了某网址。那么这样就无法实现。
如果不想发送本地dns请求就要将dns设置成空，那么就不会去本地发送dns解析。但是问题又来了，如果在自动模式下，未翻墙网址就打不开了。

综上所述：
我的意思就是说能不能设置成翻墙dns解析走远程ip，强制不发送dns本地解析。谢谢开发者！

issue_title: Add Support for Solidity Contracts Using Libraries
issue_body:
### OS / Environment
N/A
### Manticore version
0.1.8

### Python version
2.7

### Dependencies
N/A

### Summary of the problem
Manticore is currently unaware of contracts with libraries. The solidity compiler also does not appear to have a flag to inline the library code. Although it is understood that Manticore is currently designed to only analyse one contract at a time, perhaps there is a reasonable way to work with contracts that are dependent on libraries.  Another approach might be to find or write a tool that can do a source-code transformation for code that uses libraries.

### Step to reproduce the behavior
N/A

### Expected behavior
N/A

### Actual behavior
Trying to run manticore on a contract dependant on libraries results in an error when decoding that contract's bytecode, which contains the placeholders for the library addresses. 

https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/master/manticore/ethereum.py#L701

### Any relevant logs
N/A

issue_title: Elegent way to combine multiple h5 files into a single h5 file
issue_body:
Hi! Thank you for making this tool available for researchers. It is extremely helpful for data scientists and deep learning researchers. So, my problem is that I have multiple h5 files with the data as a dictionary with integer keys (0,1,...). I just want to combine these into a single file with integer keys.
for example `d1 = {0:['abc'],1:['def']}, d2={0:['qwe'],1:['rty']}`
I want `d3 = {0:['abc'],1:['def'],2:['qwe'],3:['rty']}`
is there any way to do this without loading the dictionaries into RAM and writing a script to change the keys of the dictionaries? Thank you!

My current process (that came out of manual exploratory work) is to run a number of piped csvkit tools and save the results into a file. Then load the file into pandas for further processing. Here's and example of one such chain:

```
find /mnt/logs/compress -name "cx*.csv" -print0 |
    xargs -0 csvstack --filenames |
    csvgrep -c versions -i -m "N/A" |
    csvgrep -c version -r ".+"     |
    csvcut -c group,vendor,serial,versions

```

I am moving to automate data processing and I don't want to deal with both batch scripts and python scripts. I want to have everything running in python. I already have code for enumerating files (i.e. py replacement for `find`); it's csvkit specific processing that I'm having a difficulty with.

csvkit is already written in python, but as far as I could tell there is no straight forward migration path from CLI to py.

Did I overlook something? What are the recommendations?

subproccess from python isn't an option because the script has to be runnable on various linux/windows/mac systems and I don't want to have system level dependencies outside of python (that's also why I don't want to use a batch file to run this).

issue_title: hg should not read from stderr when capturing output
issue_body:
In #104, @kergoth reported that sometimes he sees hg modules grab the wrong rev from reup:

> It seemed related to use of the particular hg modules I'm using, coupled with reup, and possibly also bound to use of the virtualenv I was using for peru. For some reason, hg was complaining about 'obsolete' markers, and peru would pick up 'obsolete' as the new rev.

I'd bet a shiny nickel that the problem is the hg plugin reading from stderr, when it should only be reading from stdout. The `capture_output=True` flag in the internals of that plugin combines the two streams, when it should probably be allowing stderr to print normally. (I think the current behavior is from before we did anything meaningful with plugin output.) Probably any condition where hg prints some random error before its usual output will hit this bug.

Description
===========

Currently CachedSchemaRegistryClient sends request to the schema registry by directly calling
get / post / put / delete methods from the requests package. This isn't flexible and provides no control over how requests are performed. Moreover this approach does not take advantage of features provided in the requests package like connection pooling for example.

My proposal is to refactor the CachedSchemaRegistryClient in the following way:
  1. Create a schema registry client which utilizes an instance of requests.Session to perform 
      requests. This instance should be provided to the registry client's constructor so that client code
      can configure it however it sees fit.
2. Build the caching schema registry client on top of that.

* Ü → [Capharnaüm](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Capharna%C3%BCm)

issue_title: map function considerably slower than pythons built-in multiprocessing map
issue_body:
I am not sure if this is an issue, but I suppose I will submit nevertheless. I have been porting some code from python's multiprocessing to pathos mainly due to pickling issue. However, it seems that pathos's map function is considerably slower than pythons built-in multiprocessing map. Consider the following simple function:

    def foo(a):
        return 2*a

    In [136]: %timeit python_pool.map(foo,range(100))
    1000 loops, best of 3: 360 µs per loop

    In [137]: %timeit pathos_pool.map(foo,range(100))
    100 loops, best of 3: 8.2 ms per loop

    In [138]: %timeit [foo(i) for i in range(100)]
    100000 loops, best of 3: 9.83 µs per loop

for bigger arrays:

    In [139]: %timeit python_pool.map(foo,range(100000))
    10 loops, best of 3: 23.1 ms per loop

    In [140]: %timeit pathos_pool.map(foo,range(100000))
    1 loops, best of 3: 883 ms per loop
    
For more complicated functions, map performs even worse. Is this expected? I am guessing the serialisation mechanism is different, but this is a huge overhead to pay for. I am using the git version of pathos
 
    pip2 install git+https://github.com/uqfoundation/pathos.git@master

issue_title: Handle change of channel state on restart gracefully
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Raiden stores serialized state of a channel on exit and uses it to restore state on startup. If the stored state differs from the contract's (blockchain) state, raiden will fail with an exception.
This situation may happen i.e.
- if contract address has changed
- if geth node is connected to a differrent network
- channel is removed from the contract for some reason
- (if user accidentally starts two nodes with the same configuration/account)

## Solution

- store contract addresses on first run inside of configuration db and warn user if the address changes
or
- derive configuration directory/db path from contract addresses . 

## Tasklist
- [ ] Discuss which approach should we use
- [ ] handle state disparity and give the user some meaningful info how to solve the situation
Create a minimal user interface using javascript and HTML. Its goal should be to allow users to access and use Raiden via a simple interface which would expose most of the API through a GUI.

issue_title: Interface comparison with emtpy __name__ reports incorrect equality
issue_body:
When comparing two different interfaces with the same, empty name they are reported to be equal:

zope.interface version 4.1.3:
```
>>> from zope.interface import Interface
>>> class A(Interface): pass
... 
>>> class B(Interface): pass
... 
>>> A == B  # correctly repots unquality
False

>>> A.__name__ = ''
>>> B.__name__ = ''
>>> A == B  # equality looks incorrect to me.
True
```

With zope.interface 3.6.7 the last test returns ``False``, so they are treated as unequal.

Also, the ``zope.interface.interface.InterfaceClass.__cmp`` function comment says ( https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.interface/blob/master/src/zope/interface/interface.py#L490 )
```
        # Yes, I did mean to name this __cmp, rather than __cmp__.
        # It is a private method used by __lt__ and __gt__.
        # I don't want to override __eq__ because I want the default
        # __eq__, which is really fast.
```
But the ``__eq__`` function was added with https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.interface/commit/c7a10ce52ad99fb6528416678fc057e5338d3e2b -- the comment should be changed.
Related: https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/570942

In [PEP-563](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0563/) a system for storing type hints is proposed and the implementation started in Python 3.7. The type hints are stored in `__annotations__`. It would be helpful to see, whether this could effect somehow the working principle of `zope.annotation`.

issue_title: ZCML principal directive should define `id` to be unicode
issue_body:
See zopefoundation/zope.security#4.

In short, the `IPrincipal` interface defines `id` to be a `TextLine`, aka `unicode` on Python 2. But the `IDefinePrincipalDirective` from this package defines `id` to be a, well, `Id`, which is a `NativeStringLine`, aka `BytesLine` on Python 2, meaning that the resulting principal objects aren't valid according to the interface.

The `zope.principalregistry.tests` contains a `principals.zcml` file with these partial contents:
```xml
  <include package="zope.principalregistry" file="meta.zcml"/>

  <principal
      id="zope.p1"
      title="Sir Tim Peters"
      description="Tim Peters"
      login="tim"
      password_manager="SHA1"
      password="40bd001563085fc35165329ea1ff5c5ecbdbbeef"
      />
```

Let's load that file:
```pycon
Python 2.7.13 (default, May  5 2017, 09:16:47)
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from zope.configuration import xmlconfig
>>> import zope.principalregistry.tests
>>> xmlconfig.file('principal.zcml', zope.principalregistry.tests)
```

Now lets get the resulting principal. We'll note that his resulting `id` is bytes (`str` since this is Python 2):
```pycon
>>> import zope.principalregistry.principalregistry
>>> prin = zope.principalregistry.principalregistry.principalRegistry.getPrincipalByLogin('tim')
>>> prin.id
'zope.p1'
>>> type(prin.id)
<type 'str'>
>>> type(prin.id) is bytes
True
```

Now this object passes the validity tests in zope.interface because it doesn't understand schemas:
```pycon
>>> from zope.security.interfaces import IPrincipal
>>> from zope.interface.verify import verifyObject
>>> verifyObject(IPrincipal, prin)
True
```

But as soon as you bring schema validation in, you see that the id is incorrect:
```pycon
>>> IPrincipal['id']
<zope.schema._bootstrapfields.TextLine object at 0x1076e9210>
>>> IPrincipal['id'].validate(prin.id)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "//site-packages/zope/schema/_bootstrapfields.py", line 183, in validate
    self._validate(value)
  File "//site-packages/zope/schema/_bootstrapfields.py", line 310, in _validate
    super(MinMaxLen, self)._validate(value)
  File "//site-packages/zope/schema/_bootstrapfields.py", line 210, in _validate
    raise WrongType(value, self._type, self.__name__)
zope.schema._bootstrapinterfaces.WrongType: ('zope.p1', <type 'unicode'>, 'id')
```

issue_title: RFC checker: support a more powerful configuration file format
issue_body:
ConfigParser and friends do not natively support any advanced types, forcing us to do things like [use RawConfigParser to actually get what the user put in the file](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/modules/bibcheck/lib/bibcheck_task.py#L560) and then proceed to [manually replicate SafeConfigParser behaviour](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/modules/bibcheck/lib/bibcheck_task.py#L531). It also makes validating the configuration files more tedious than it has to be.

Supporting a format like YAML (with PyYAML) and a validator (like pyKwalify) can help with problems like these. YAML natively supports more types, including dicts and nested lists. It can return class instances directly from the data, or even load code to allow administrators to extend functionality a little bit more. It also remains highly human readable and writable.

issue_title: Multiple RSA server certificates not allowed
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-08-03

Invenio creates apache conf that will not work if there are existing virtual hosts because only one ssl certificate is allowed per IP.

The solution is to move the ssl directive *inside* the <VirtualHost>, eg.

<VirtualHost *:9443>
        SSLCertificateFile /etc/apache2/ssl/server.crt
        SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/apache2/ssl/server.key
        ....
</VirtualHost>
Flask-Babelex should probably be added as installation dependency, just like other modules: https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-oauthclient/blob/master/setup.py#L70

Issue: On mac when redis is run in a docker machine the host ip is the one of the virtual box vm, not localhost. The default value of ACCOUNTS_SESSION_REDIS_URL is hard coded (https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/blob/434f56e5c10bf2f7f996d03880c43ff2e094a08b/invenio_accounts/config.py#L29). Thus docker cannot be used when running tests on a mac.

Solution: load ACCOUNTS_SESSION_REDIS_URL from environment if it is set. This solution is already used in invenio-search (https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-search/blob/f19a3c743ad9d578d0840a416e7dd76e6fffca06/invenio_search/config.py#L58)

The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).
Add support for adding decorators to views. Most importantly, we need to be able to add access restrictions.

issue_title: views: adapt other modules to the new suggesters structure
issue_body:
Suggesters are now compatible with ES v2 and v5, such that the ``_source`` will be available in the suggestion response, and should be used as the source of information. The previously used fields such as ``output`` and ``payload`` are no longer available in ES5, hence all UI elements which use suggesters should rely only on ``_source``.

This requires changes in indexing receivers signals, and in case of generic invenio modules, the suggesters have to be indexed two-way (ES2 and ES5 style), see for example: https://github.com/nharraud/invenio-records-rest/commit/aade39fe0ba3dc8c9719c8ea5b9785a6b09091f6#diff-484462fced51d1a06b1d93b4a44dd535R236

I have a Jupyter notebook that I'm testing widgets in and found that the new DatePicker widget doesn't work in Safari, but works fine in Chrome.  Here's the offending code:

DatePickerView.prototype._update_value = function () {

issue_title: Can the tab bar wrap horizontally?
issue_body:
I have a tab widget that contains more children that can fit in usual window width. Each tab contains a bunch of check boxes in a Box widget. I can control the layout of the tab contents, so the check boxes wrap around, but I can't seem to control the layout of the tabs header (tab bar?), which scrolls horizontally. Is there a way to make the tab bar wrap horizontally, so the user doesn't have to scroll to select the tab? 

```
import ipywidgets as widgets

class TabsNotWrapping: 
    def __init__(self, available_factors):
        self.available_factors = available_factors
        self.layout = widgets.Layout(display='flex', flex_flow='row wrap')
        self.tabs = self.tabs_multi_checkbox(available_factors)

    def tabs_multi_checkbox(self, items):
        descriptions = ['_'.join([i, str(c)]) for i in items for c in range(10)]
        options_dict = {description: widgets.Checkbox(
            description=description, value=False, style = {'description_width': 'initial'}) 
                        for description in descriptions}
        options = [options_dict[description] for description in descriptions]
        tabs = widgets.Tab(children=[
            widgets.Box([options_dict[d] for d in descriptions if d.startswith(i)], layout=self.layout) 
            for i in items
        ])
        for c, f in enumerate(items):
            tabs.set_title(c, f)

        return tabs

items = ['Abong-Mbang', 'Ako', 'Akonolinga', 'Ambam', 'Bafang', 'Bafia', 'Bafoussam', 'Bafut', 'Bagangte', 
         'Bali', 'Bamenda', 'Bamingie', 'Nkwen', 'Bandjoun', 'Bangem', 'Banyo', 'Batouri', 'Bélabo', 'Bertoua', 'Buea']
tmp = TabsNotWrapping(items)
display(tmp.tabs)
```

Here is the output. You can see how the tabs' content wraps around, but the tabs' header doesn't.

I tried to specify the tab's layout to be the same as the tab's content, but it didn't change anything:
```
        tabs = widgets.Tab(children=[
            widgets.Box([options_dict[d] for d in descriptions if d.startswith(i)], layout=self.layout) 
            for i in items
        ], layout=self.layout)

```

Setup:

- Jupyter notebook 5.0.0

Thank you.

Currently `fields.Nested` assumes that the value of the field is a list - https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/dev/marshmallow/schema.py#L702 - and fails for `set` during serialization

First of all thanks a lot for the nice library to all contributors. We are using as our main validator of all our API input.
 
This issue describes a concern about a common `pre_load` pitfall  (imho) that leads to breaking code.

`pre_load` is used in all examples as a place to clean up data. But essentially to do a safe clean up, one needs to validate the type and the existence of the key most of the times.
For example in the following example that is from the documentation 
```
from marshmallow import Schema, fields, pre_load

class UserSchema(Schema):
    name = fields.Str()
    slug = fields.Str()

    @pre_load
    def slugify_name(self, in_data):
        in_data['slug'] = in_data['slug'].lower().strip().replace(' ', '-')
        return in_data
```
the following will break without validation error
```
schema = UserSchema().load({'name': 'Steve'})
```
I do not have a clear suggestion but it feels quite problematic and error prone. I our case we are doing in all the cases we use `pre_load` for accessing string fields
```
try:
   ....
except (KeyError, AttributeError):
    pass
```
which is not very elegant. 
Maybe stressing this out in the documentation would be enough.

issue_title: Django >= 1.8 ; Deprecated OptParser usage ArgParse; Warnings on running runserver_plus
issue_body:
Running `runserver_plus` with `Werkzeug` debugger throws the following warning with the latest Django

```
RemovedInDjango110Warning: OptionParser usage for Django management commands is deprecated, use ArgumentParser instead
  RemovedInDjango110Warning)
```

Looks like this is happening because of the usage of `*args` and `**options`,  See the changed in 1.8 which says https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.9/howto/custom-management-commands/#module-django.core.management
```
Changed in Django 1.8:
Before Django 1.8, management commands were based on the optparse module, and positional 
arguments were passed in *args while optional arguments were passed in **options. Now that 
management commands use argparse for argument parsing, all arguments are passed in **options 
by default, unless you name your positional arguments to args (compatibility mode). You are 
encouraged to exclusively use **options for new commands.
```

RFC: new driver for Cisco 350 series devices
issue_body:
I've been working on a [driver for Cisco 350 series devices](https://github.com/jasperla/napalm-sx350) though I'd like to confirm the name before requesting moving it to [napalm-automation-community](https://github.com/napalm-automation-community).

Now, the datasheet or other forms of documentation are not very clear on which operating system devices run. Based on what's printed when the device boots and inspection of the binary firmware image itself, it seems some form of Linux:

```
Linux MARVELL_LINUX 3.10.70 #2 SMP Sun Aug 20 19:04:38 IDT 2017


MAC Address   :  xx.

Autoboot in 2 seconds - press RETURN or Esc. to abort and enter prom.

*******************************************************************
*** Running  SW  Ver. 2.3.5.63  Date Aug 20 2017  Time 19:03:04 ***
*******************************************************************

HW version is V02
Base Mac address is: xx
Dram size is  : 512M bytes
Dram first block size is  : 370688K bytes
Dram first PTR is  : 0x89600000
Dram second block size is  : 4096K bytes
Dram second PTR is  : 0x19000000
Flash size is: 256M
20-Aug-2017 06:28:08 %CDB-I-LOADCONFIG: Loading running configuration.
20-Aug-2017 06:28:09 %CDB-I-LOADCONFIG: Loading startup configuration.
Device configuration:
Slot 1 - SG350-10
Device 0: GT_98DX3233 (AlleyCat3)
CPLD version is: 0x0e
```

This is quite different from the previous Cisco 300 series which ran a vxworks-based firmware. So I think the best approach is to name the driver according to how upstream references it in the docs: Sx350. Right?

NB, while the interface is not too dissimilar from IOS, it is actually too different in order to make it work with the existing IOS driver without turning it into one big `if sx350/else` construct.

issue_title: Host list on service edition form - template mode
issue_body: When editing a service template, the host list should be replaced with the hosts templates list ... this to allow attaching a service template to an host template
